The first RAG reserve is archived and supplies no unique selected stratum; the next canonical active RAG reserve is used without consulting detector output.

Metadata resolution found three selected repositories excluded as archived and exhausted the existing agent_tools reserves. Before any detector execution or result, the policy was versioned and re-locked with an explicit exhausted-reserve extension rule; thresholds and detector rules did not change.

Under llm-selection-v1.1, browser-use/browser-use was appended at reserve order 9 as the first metadata-eligible active agent_tools repository after existing same-surface reserves were consumed. No detector was run or consulted.
AgentGPT is archived and supplies no unique stratum. The first remaining eligible canonical agent_tools reserve is e2b-dev/fragments; no detector output was consulted.
TaskWeaver is archived and supplies no unique stratum. The next eligible agent_tools reserve is browser-use/browser-use; no detector output was consulted.
h2ogpt is archived and supplies no unique stratum. The first eligible canonical RAG reserve after archived NVIDIA/ChatRTX is mem0ai/mem0; no detector output was consulted.

An isolated AI reviewer disclosed first-pass untouched source opportunities and labels to the rule-authoring orchestrator before formal detector freeze. No detector had run and no detector rule changed in response, but the intended blindness boundary could no longer be claimed. The immutable v1.1 manifest and private labels remain preserved as correction evidence and are excluded from measurement.

Two distinct AI reviewers independently searched GitHub metadata without cloning or labeling source and without detector access. Canonical proposal SHA-256 values are 1968ca8ad6ef58f42f74ff64dcb690a12b88a110869a6df2987b69203203283e and c0232743619ddc7ded9c7252acb098fcb4abc0988ff869ff138e6d4e6f92b137.

The selector source d4712babd2a50b2f8fee44b18e13e0aec4704e9713d0eff2ee224e782a14a171 bound the exact 53-ID exclusion set, incident record, proposals, surface quotas, provider seeds, and candidate document. Selection record byte SHA-256 is b758cbfd75f7cd9aad839d5f75882c3f86ee34c59c49ee55df25193e8b5cf848; canonical self-hash is d81190691bff5001399dce5bcae3bf08f2a4e3926e12731b90b5d0b72cb55aa0.

Two disclosed independent AI review records approved only the exact replacement bytes after live metadata checks and two deterministic replays. Public review-record byte SHA-256 values are e8b88b63b5454c2266bd39e97d90f4e21b27026ee004663000d8794f111f8012 and d2e0cf88ba95f2582d4f2dfe1c0462de5f94536886f6adafb0d879ca90549446; they are not represented as human review.

Before any detector execution, the blinding incident was disclosed, the original untouched cohort was retired, and a completely disjoint replacement was selected and twice reviewed from metadata only. Release thresholds and detector rules did not change.

Two isolated AI tasks independently searched GitHub metadata without cloning or labeling source and without detector-result access. Selector source bfdee731e0834025402772ef85db9b8db5fe66a5c534317477d9dad343cff12d bound the exclusion set, v1.2 integrity NO-GO record, proposals, exact surface quotas, provider seeds, and candidate document. The v1.2 evidence cycle ended in an integrity NO-GO and its untouched cohort is spent. Before v1.3 detector execution, a completely disjoint cohort was selected from two isolated metadata-only searches. Release thresholds did not change.
